Package nonprofitbookkeeping.model

Class InventoryItem

java.lang.Object
nonprofitbookkeeping.model.InventoryItem
public class InventoryItem extends Object
Represents an inventory item, typically a fixed asset, subject to depreciation. This class stores details about the item's cost, acquisition date, useful life, and depreciation information. Lombok's @Data, @AllArgsConstructor, and @NoArgsConstructor are used for boilerplate code generation.

  • Constructor Summary

    Constructors

    Description
    InventoryItem(String id, String name, BigDecimal cost, String acquired, int lifeYears)
    Constructs an InventoryItem with essential details.

  • Constructor Details

    • InventoryItem

      public InventoryItem(String id, String name, BigDecimal cost, String acquired, int lifeYears)
      Constructs an InventoryItem with essential details. Initializes depreciationRate to null and depreciationMethod to "Straight-Line" by default. Accumulated depreciation and net value are not set by this constructor initially.
      Parameters:
      id - The unique identifier for the item.
      name - The name of the item.
      cost - The original cost of the item.
      acquired - The acquisition date of the item (e.g., "YYYY-MM-DD").
      lifeYears - The estimated useful life of the item in years.

  • Method Details

    • getId

      public String getId()
      Gets the unique identifier of the inventory item.
      Returns:
      The item ID.

    • getName

      public String getName()
      Gets the name of the inventory item.
      Returns:
      The item name.

    • getAcquiredDate

      public String getAcquiredDate()
      Gets the acquisition date of the inventory item.
      Returns:
      The acquisition date as a string (e.g., "YYYY-MM-DD").

    • getCost

      public BigDecimal getCost()
      Gets the original cost of the inventory item.
      Returns:
      The item cost.

    • getAccumulatedDepreciation

      public BigDecimal getAccumulatedDepreciation()
      Gets the accumulated depreciation of the inventory item. This value is typically set via withAccumDep(BigDecimal) or a direct setter.
      Returns:
      The accumulated depreciation amount.

    • getNetBookValue

      public BigDecimal getNetBookValue()
      Gets the net book value of the inventory item. This value is calculated as Cost - Accumulated Depreciation.
      Returns:
      The net book value.

    • getLifeYears

      public int getLifeYears()
      Gets the estimated useful life of the inventory item in years.
      Returns:
      The useful life in years.

    • withAccumDep

      public InventoryItem withAccumDep(BigDecimal accDep1)
      Sets the accumulated depreciation for this item and recalculates its net book value. This method provides a fluent interface.
      Parameters:
      accDep1 - The accumulated depreciation amount to set.
      Returns:
      This InventoryItem instance for chaining.

    • setAccumulatedDepreciation

      public void setAccumulatedDepreciation(BigDecimal valueOf)
      Sets the accumulated depreciation for the item. Note: This is a stub method. The @Data annotation from Lombok should provide this setter. Consider using withAccumDep(BigDecimal) for recalculating net value.
      Parameters:
      valueOf - The accumulated depreciation amount to set.

    • setDepreciationRate

      public void setDepreciationRate(BigDecimal valueOf)
      Sets the depreciation rate for the item. Note: This is a stub method. The @Data annotation from Lombok should provide this setter.
      Parameters:
      valueOf - The depreciation rate to set (e.g., 0.10 for 10%).

    • getDepreciationMethod

      public String getDepreciationMethod()
      Returns the configured depreciation method, if any.
      Returns:
      the depreciation method name or null

    • getDepreciationRate

      public BigDecimal getDepreciationRate()
      Gets the depreciation rate of the item. Note: This is a stub method and currently returns null. The @Data annotation from Lombok should provide this getter.
      Returns:
      The depreciation rate, or null if not set or if stub is not replaced.

    • setDepreciationMethod

      public void setDepreciationMethod(String method)
      Sets the depreciation method for the item. Note: This is a stub method. The @Data annotation from Lombok should provide this setter.
      Parameters:
      method - The depreciation method to set (e.g., "Straight-Line").

    • getNetValue

      public BigDecimal getNetValue()
      Gets the net book value of the item. Note: This is a stub method and currently returns null. The @Data annotation from Lombok should provide a getter for 'netValue'. The actual calculation is typically Cost - Accumulated Depreciation.
      Returns:
      The net book value, or null if not calculated or if stub is not replaced.
